title: types-parser-foundation
body:
## Parent Story
#2

## Complexity
medium

## Files
src/lib.rs, src/types.rs, src/parser.rs, tests/parser_test.rs

---

## Overview

Implement core types and dice notation parser for the sage-dice engine.

## Core Types

Create `src/types.rs` with these exact definitions:

```rust
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum DiceSides {
    Standard(u32),
    Fate,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum Modifier {
    Add(i32),
    Subtract(i32),
    KeepHighest(u32),
    KeepLowest(u32),
    DropHighest(u32),
    DropLowest(u32),
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct DiceExpression {
    pub count: u32,
    pub sides: DiceSides,
    pub modifiers: Vec<Modifier>,
    pub exploding: bool,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ExplosionChain {
    pub values: Vec<i32>,
    pub total: i32,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct RollResult {
    pub expression: DiceExpression,
    pub rolls: Vec<i32>,
    pub kept: Vec<i32>,
    pub dropped: Vec<i32>,
    pub modifier_total: i32,
    pub total: i32,
    pub explosions: Vec<ExplosionChain>,
    pub fate_ladder: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, thiserror::Error)]
pub enum DiceError {
    #[error("Parse error: {0}")]
    ParseError(String),
    #[error("Invalid dice notation: {0}")]
    InvalidNotation(String),
    #[error("Roll error: {0}")]
    RollError(String),
}
```

## Parser Implementation

Create `src/parser.rs` with recursive descent parser:

```rust
use crate::types::{DiceExpression, DiceSides, Modifier, DiceError};

pub fn parse(input: &str) -> Result<DiceExpression, DiceError> {
    let input = input.trim().to_lowercase();
    if input.is_empty() {
        return Err(DiceError::ParseError("empty input".to_string()));
    }
    // Implement recursive descent parser
    todo!()
}
```

## Parser Behavior

### Supported Formats (Story 1)
- "d20" → 1d20 (count defaults to 1)
- "2d6" → 2d6
- "2d6+3" → 2d6 with Add(3) modifier
- "1d20-2" → 1d20 with Subtract(2) modifier
- "4d6kh3" → 4d6 with KeepHighest(3)
- "2d20kl1" → 2d20 with KeepLowest(1)
- "4d6dl1" → 4d6 with DropLowest(1)
- "4d6dh1" → 4d6 with DropHighest(1)
- Case-insensitive (2D6+3 = 2d6+3)
- Whitespace trimmed

### D9 Edge Cases (MUST ENFORCE)
- "0d6" → Error (zero dice)
- "d0" → Error (zero sides)
- "d1" → Valid (always rolls 1)
- "999999d6" → Error (exceeds 10000 dice limit)
- "d999999" → Error (exceeds 10000 sides limit)
- "10000d10000" → Valid (at limit)
- "4d6kh10" → Valid (parser accepts; engine keeps all 4)
- "4d6kh0" → Error (keep zero)
- "2d6++3" → Error (malformed)
- "d20k" → Error (incomplete keep)

## Library Exports

Update `src/lib.rs`:

```rust
pub mod types;
pub mod parser;

pub use types::*;
pub use parser::parse;
```

## Tests

Create `tests/parser_test.rs` with all test cases from story spec:

- test_parse_simple_d20
- test_parse_2d6
- test_parse_with_modifier
- test_parse_negative_modifier
- test_parse_keep_highest
- test_parse_keep_lowest
- test_parse_drop_lowest
- test_parse_drop_highest
- test_parse_case_insensitive
- test_parse_zero_dice_error
- test_parse_zero_sides_error
- test_parse_one_sided_allowed
- test_parse_overflow_dice_error
- test_parse_overflow_sides_error
- test_parse_at_limit_allowed
- test_parse_keep_more_than_rolled
- test_parse_keep_zero_error
- test_parse_malformed_double_plus_error
- test_parse_incomplete_keep_error
- test_parse_invalid
- test_parse_whitespace_handling

## Grammar

```
expr     = roll modifiers*
roll     = number? "d" number
modifiers = (("+" | "-") number) | keep
keep     = ("kh" | "kl" | "dh" | "dl") number
number   = [0-9]+
```

## Success Criteria

- `cargo build` succeeds
- All parser tests pass
- Parser enforces D9 edge cases
- Types match spec exactly
- No thiserror compilation errors

---

## Implementation Context

This chunk contains everything needed for implementation. The spec has been
validated for completeness before reaching you.

If you find yourself wanting to search the codebase or read files not
mentioned here, that's signal the spec is incomplete. Output:
`CHUNK_BLOCKED: <what information is missing>`

Trust the spec. Implement what's described.
